root_text.an4.235:1.1: “Cattārimāni, bhikkhave, kammāni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditāni.
root_text.an4.235:1.6: atthi, bhikkhave, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.235:1.8: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco pāṇātipātī hoti, adinnādāyī hoti, kāmesumicchācārī hoti, musāvādī hoti, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhāyī hoti.
root_text.an4.235:1.9: Idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ kaṇhaṁ kaṇhavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.235:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti, adinnādānā paṭivirato hoti, kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato hoti, musāvādā paṭivirato hoti, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato hoti.
root_text.an4.235:2.3: Idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ sukkaṁ sukkavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.235:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti …pe… idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ kaṇhasukkaṁ kaṇhasukkavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.235:4.1: Katamañca, bhikkhave, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati?
root_text.an4.235:4.2: Tatra, bhikkhave, yamidaṁ kammaṁ kaṇhaṁ kaṇhavipākaṁ …pe… idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.235:4.3: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, cattāri kammāni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditānī”ti.

translation_text.an4.235:1.1: “Mendicants, I declare these four kinds of deeds, having realized them with my own insight.
translation_text.an4.235:1.3: There are dark deeds with dark results;
translation_text.an4.235:1.5: dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results; and
translation_text.an4.235:1.6: neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds.
translation_text.an4.235:1.7: And what are the dark deeds with dark results?
translation_text.an4.235:1.8: It’s when someone kills living creatures, steals, commits sexual misconduct, lies, and consumes beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an4.235:1.9: These are called dark deeds with dark results.
translation_text.an4.235:2.1: And what are bright deeds with bright results?
translation_text.an4.235:2.2: It’s when someone doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an4.235:2.3: These are called bright deeds with bright results.
translation_text.an4.235:3.1: And what are dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results?
translation_text.an4.235:3.2: It’s when someone makes both hurtful and pleasing choices by way of body, speech, and mind. These are called dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results.
translation_text.an4.235:4.1: And what are neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds?
translation_text.an4.235:4.2: It’s the intention to give up dark deeds with dark results, bright deeds with bright results, and both dark and bright deeds with both dark and bright results. These are called neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds.
translation_text.an4.235:4.3: These are the four kinds of deeds that I declare, having realized them with my own insight.”
